chunk_text:
1 Check program

MachineDataCheck program
Entry-ID: 109738423, V100, 05/2026 4

© Siemens AG Copyright year All rights reserved
1 Check program
1.1 Overview
For verification of settings of a SINUMERIK control regarding different functions, there is a
check program MachineDataCheck program (short form MDC).

It is essential that the program matchs the respective software version installed on the machine.
The result of the check is written to a MDC_RESULT.MPF file in the MPF.DIR folder.
1.2 Mode of operation
Primary this subprogram shall help to set the machine data for AdvancedSurface,
TopSurface, TopSpeed (plus) and Advanced Rapid Movement as recommended.
The subprogram checks the machine and setting data and writes an information about
settings which are different to the recommended values.

doc_title: SINUMERIK Check Program for TOP Surface Settings
chunk_text:
ecommended.
The subprogram checks the machine and setting data and writes an information about
settings which are different to the recommended values.

Additionally, the subprogram checks if the relevant G-Codes relevant for the specific function
have the required or recommended value.
Usually, these values shall be set by CYCLE832.SPF and CUST_832.SPF. To check these
values, the subprogram has to be executed after calling CYCLE832.

The subprogram creates the protocol file MDC_RESULT.MPF in the same directory where the
MDC is located.

Please check if MD N11420 $MN_PROTOCOL_LEN is set >= 10 (kB).

doc_title: SINUMERIK Check Program for TOP Surface Settings
chunk_text:
reates the protocol file MDC_RESULT.MPF in the same directory where the
MDC is located.

Please check if MD N11420 $MN_PROTOCOL_LEN is set >= 10 (kB).

Define the type of the check by variable CHECK at the beginning of the MDC:
- 0 = no check,
- 1 = only for AdvancedSurface
- 2 = for AdvancedSurface and TopSurface
- 3 = for AdvancedSurface and TopSurface with tool radius compensation CUT3D*
- 4 = for AdvancedSurface, TopSurface and TopSpeed / TopSpeedPlus
- 5 = for AdvancedSurface, TopSurface and TopSpeed / TopSpeedPlus with
tool radius compensation CUT3D*
- 6 = for AdvancedRapidMovement (COMPPATH)

And please define the type of machine tool - necessary for machine tools only with three axes
for interpolation - in the variable FLAG_3AX
TRUE: machine tool has only three interpolation axes
FALSE: machine tool has more than 3 interpolation axes

At the end of the subprogram a message is displayed about the state of AdvancedSurface or
TopSurface and the active tolerance values.
